title: Microcoelia conica
taxonomy: Microcoelia conica is a species of mycoheterotrophic orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. Mycoheterotrophic orchids are known for obtaining nutrients by parasitizing fungi. Microcoelia conica is native to tropical regions of Africa. This species is also known as a saprophyte, as it obtains its nutrients from decomposing organic matter.

intro: Microcoelia conica is a species of epiphytic orchid, characterized by its unique, conical-shaped pseudobulb. The plant has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Africa, for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Microcoelia conica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties.
